content: TIER 2 — THE ADULT WORLD OPENS
Tier 1 gave you the vocabulary of clear
personal expression. Tier 2 gives you the
vocabulary of civic and professional life.
This is the language found on forms, in the,
news, in contracts, and in arguments. The
words that allow you to read a policy
document and understand it clearly.
Ten clusters encompassing Two hundred words.
Government, work, finance, health, media,
law, education, and society.
These words govern your life.
Apply them skillfully.
Synonyms often are not interchangable:
It can be a trap: your thesaurus says
'big' and 'vast' are synonyms.
But they aren't. Not really.
'Big problem'    — fine.
'Vast problem'   — almost right, but
slightly off.
'Vast landscape' — yes.
'Big landscape'  — technically correct,
somehow wrong.
Every synonym pair has a fingerprint.
Same rough meaning. Different weight,
different context, different feel.
The difference between a good writer and
talented one, is to observe one habit:
Ask 'is this the right word, or is it
simply a word that's close enough?'
Close enough works in speech, but in
written English, it pays to be precise.
Readers notice inprecise use of language,
when they can't say exactly what's wrong.
Tier 3 introduces the vocabulary of clear
thinking. Logic, cause and effect, nuance.
The words that structure arguments and
mark careful reasoning.
These words don't just describe ideas.
They organise them. Good luck!
